overview.location: Unincorporated San Mateo County, California — 12 miles south of downtown San Francisco
overview.passengerVolume: 54.5 million passengers per year (2025) — 13th busiest in the U.S., 2nd busiest in California after LAX
overview.distinction: Primary airport for the San Francisco Bay Area. Major hub for United Airlines. One of the first U.S. airports with direct BART rail connection.
overview.operator: City and County of San Francisco (Airport Commission)
overview.runwayLength: 7,650 ft to 11,870 ft — two sets of parallel runways with only 750 ft separation (capacity constraint in poor visibility)
overview.dominantAirline: United Airlines (largest hub, operating from Terminal 3 and International Terminal)
overview.securityProvider: Covenant Aviation Security (private contractor, not TSA — SFO is one of ~20 U.S. airports using private screeners under TSA oversight)

terminals.name: Harvey Milk Terminal 1 (Boarding Areas B & C)
terminals.notes: Named after Harvey Milk since 2019. Boarding Areas B and C. New terminal features extensive art collection, local restaurant concessions, and sustainability features (LEED Gold). Southwest Airlines is the dominant carrier here.

terminals.name: Terminal 2 (Boarding Area D)
terminals.status: Active — renovated 2011 ($383 million), LEED Gold certified
terminals.notes: Smallest terminal. Connected to Terminal 1 and Terminal 3 post-security. First U.S. airport terminal to achieve LEED Gold status. Houses the SkyTerrace observation deck (opened February 2020). Known for local food options and sustainability focus.

terminals.name: Terminal 3 (Boarding Areas E & F)
terminals.status: Active — Terminal 3 West undergoing $2.6 billion renovation (completion expected 2029)
terminals.notes: United Airlines domestic hub. Boarding Areas E and F. Terminal 3 East reopened January 2014 ($138 million renovation). Terminal 3 West renovation began 2024 — will add new gates, wider concourses, and improved passenger amenities. United Club lounges in both boarding areas. Solar roof installed 2007.

terminals.name: International Terminal (Boarding Areas A & G)
terminals.gates: 29 gates (A1-A15, G1-G14) — six gates can accommodate Airbus A380
terminals.notes: Boarding Areas A and G. Largest international terminal in North America when opened. Largest building in the world built on base isolators for earthquake protection. Houses BART station on Level 3. SFO Museum gallery with rotating exhibitions (American Alliance of Museums accredited since 1999). Multiple international airline lounges. Global Entry and TSA PreCheck available.

runways.features: Category III ILS equipped — primary instrument approach runway

runwayOperations.westPlan: Used ~81% of the time — Runways 1L/1R for departures, 28L/28R for arrivals
runwayOperations.southeastPlan: Used ~4% of the time — Runways 10L/10R for departures, 19L/19R for arrivals
runwayOperations.parallelSeparation: 750 feet centerline to centerline (unusually close — key capacity constraint)
runwayOperations.clearWeatherCapacity: ~60 arrivals per hour with simultaneous parallel approaches (NOW RESTRICTED — see 2026 FAA changes)
runwayOperations.poorVisibilityCapacity: 25-30 arrivals per hour (single-runway instrument approaches required)

authority: San Francisco Airport Commission (City and County of San Francisco)

bart.station: SFO BART Station — Level 3 of International Terminal
bart.payment: Contactless bank cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay, or Clipper card. Paper tickets no longer sold.
bart.frequency: Combined Yellow + Red Line: every ~10 minutes to downtown SF (weekday peak). After 9 PM: every ~20 minutes. Weekends: every ~20 minutes.
bart.connection: Free AirTrain from all terminals to BART station (3-5 min ride)
bart.caltrainTransfer: Transfer at Millbrae station (4-min BART ride from SFO). Caltrain zone fares: $3.45 Clipper/$4.00 cash to SF or Palo Alto, $5.70/$6.25 to San Jose Diridon. Two of four Caltrain trains per hour are timed with BART.
bart.lateNightNote: Sunday BART does not start until 8:08 AM — early morning flights require alternative transport. Late-night Millbrae shuttle runs every 15 min after 9 PM.
bart.constructionAlert: Train control modernization: expect 30-minute delays some Sunday-Thursday nights between Millbrae and SFO through Summer 2026.

lines.redLine: Clockwise loop (~9 min): Terminal 2 → Terminal 3 (CLOSED for renovation, use Terminal 2 station) → International G → Garage G/BART → International A → Harvey Milk Terminal 1 → Garage A → Grand Hyatt at SFO
lines.blueLine: Counterclockwise loop (~25 min): All Red Line stops PLUS West Field Road, Rental Car Center, and Long-Term Parking. Blue Line is the ONLY way to reach Rental Car Center and Long-Term Parking.

airTrain.terminal3Closure: Terminal 3 AirTrain station closed since November 4, 2025 for Terminal 3 West renovation. Use 'Terminals 2 & 3' station and walk. Reopening expected 2027.
airTrain.funding: Funded by rental car customer facility charges

taxi.sfoPickupSurcharge: $6.00 (100% collected from passenger; NO surcharge for drop-offs)
taxi.luggageFee: Up to $1.00/piece if it cannot fit in trunk/passenger compartment
taxi.outOfTownTrips: 150% of metered rate for trips exceeding 15 miles beyond SF city limits

estimatedFares.toSanJose: ~$100-$130 (150% meter applies beyond 15 mi from SF)

taxi.flatRateOption: YoTaxi App: $35 flat rate to SFO from anywhere in SF. Shared ride flat rate: up to $20/person (requires advance consent).
taxi.pickup: Taxi stands on Arrivals/lower level at every terminal, curbside outside baggage claim

estimatedFares.toDowntownSF: $25-$40 UberX/Lyft ($55-$85+ Uber Black/Lyft Lux). Surge can push standard to $60-80+

rideshare.airportFee: $3.80 airport surcharge per pickup or drop-off (included in app fare)

pickupLocations.domesticTerminals: Level 5 of Domestic Parking Garage — follow signs and walk across connecting sky-bridges from Arrivals. Request ride AFTER arriving at Level 5.
pickupLocations.internationalTerminal: Center island of Departures-level roadway (upper level), curbside

rideshare.premiumPickup: Uber Black SUV and WAV (wheelchair accessible) pick up curbside at domestic terminals. All other services (including Uber Black sedan) are Level 5.
rideshare.waymo: Waymo autonomous vehicles began operating at SFO in January 2026
rideshare.driverRequirement: All rideshare drivers must display light blue SFO Airport placard (effective May 1, 2025)
rideshare.waitTimes: Typical: 3-8 min daytime, 10-15+ min late night/early morning

routes.route292: San Francisco – Daly City – South SF – SFO – Millbrae – Burlingame – San Mateo (primary SFO route, weekday/Saturday/Sunday)
routes.route397: San Francisco – SFO – Palo Alto Transit Center (express)
routes.route120: Colma – SFO (one daily run, 4:41 AM weekdays)

samTrans.pickup: Terminal 2 at Courtyard 2, Terminal 3 at Courtyard 4, International Terminal at Courtyard A/G
samTrans.note: SamTrans provides around-the-clock SFO service via combination of daytime routes and ECR OWL overnight

superShuttle.pricing: $17-$23/person shared ride van to downtown SF

rentalCars.location: Rental Car Center — 780 N. McDonnell Road, San Francisco, CA 94128
rentalCars.connection: Free AirTrain Blue Line from all terminals to Rental Car Center
rentalCars.offSiteAccess: Free shuttle service from the south curb of the Rental Car Center upon customer request

parkingRates: On-airport: Domestic Garage $39/day, International Garage $37/day, Long-Term Garage $27/day, ParkFAST $60/day, ParkVALET $45/day. Off-site: from $8/day with free shuttle. Cell Phone Waiting Lot: free (up to 60 minutes). BART from downtown SF: $8.95 one-way (~30 min).

domesticGarage.features: Direct elevator access to terminals. EV charging available in Green Vehicle Parking Zones.

internationalGarage.features: Direct access to International Terminal. EV charging in Green Vehicle Parking Zones.

longTermGarage.address: 794 South Airport Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94128
longTermGarage.connection: Free AirTrain Blue Line to all terminals (Level 5 access)
longTermGarage.features: Most affordable on-airport option. Best for trips of 3+ days.

parkFast.features: Premium reserved spots closest to terminal entrances. Guaranteed spot.

parkValet.features: Drop off your car at the terminal curbside, valet parks it for you. No online reservations.

cellPhoneLot.location: North Access Road, near airport entrance
cellPhoneLot.rules: Maximum 60 minutes. Driver must remain with vehicle.

evCharging.overview: SFO provides complimentary Level 1 and Level 2 EV charging across all public garages in designated Green Vehicle Parking Zones. Charging is free (included with parking fee) on a first-come, first-served basis. Managed by ChargePoint network.

longTermGarage.levels2to6: 6 Level 1 stalls per floor near elevators (30 total)
longTermGarage.note: Level 2 ChargePoint stations also available — 4th floor of Garage 2 has up to 104 ports

surfaceLot.stalls: Level 1 charging adjacent to Bus Stops 5, 7, and 8

parkValetService.location: Level 4 of Domestic Garage — plug-in EV charging for valet customers

evCharging.totalEstimatedPorts: 85+ Level 1/Level 2 ports across all garages, plus 104 ChargePoint Level 2 ports in Long-Term Garage 2

chargingTypes.level1: Standard 120V outlet — bring your own cable. Adds ~4-5 miles of range per hour.
chargingTypes.level2: 240V ChargePoint stations — J1772 connector. Adds ~25 miles of range per hour.

evCharging.cost: Free with paid parking. No separate charging fee.
evCharging.findStations: Search 'SFO' on chargepoint.com or ChargePoint app for real-time availability

skychargerHub.hardware: Kempower — 12 Power Cabinets and 24 Satellite dispensers with dynamic power distribution
skychargerHub.targetUsers: Electric rideshare drivers (Uber/Lyft), airport guests, and general public
skychargerHub.context: Supports California's Clean Miles Standard — 90% of Uber/Lyft miles must be EV by 2030

evCharging.dcFastCharging: No public DC fast charging currently at SFO garages. The Skycharger Hub (24 ports, 400 kW) will be the first when completed.

onAirport.location: International Terminal, Main Hall, Level 3, near G Gates

pricing.note: Less than 24 hours still charged the 24-hour rate

onAirport.additionalServices: Bag wrapping, travel insurance, passport photos, notary public, photocopies, faxing, showers

offSiteAlternatives.name: Bounce (formerly usebounce.com)
offSiteAlternatives.locations: Partner shops and hotels near SFO and in downtown SF
offSiteAlternatives.booking: Online booking required — app or website
offSiteAlternatives.hours: Varies by location (many open 7 AM - 10 PM)

offSiteAlternatives.name: LuggageHero
offSiteAlternatives.locations: 1000+ partner locations in San Francisco (hotels, shops, cafes)
offSiteAlternatives.note: Hourly pricing is unique — good for short layovers

offSiteAlternatives.name: Radical Storage
offSiteAlternatives.locations: Multiple partner locations in San Francisco

offSiteAlternatives.name: Nannybag
offSiteAlternatives.locations: Partner businesses and hotels near SFO and throughout SF

luggageStorage.comparison: Airport Travel Agency ($3-$15/day) is convenient but limited to International Terminal hours. Off-site services like Nannybag ($2.50/day) and LuggageHero ($1.49/hr) are cheaper but require travel to partner locations in the city. For quick airport layovers, the on-airport option is most practical.

petReliefAreas.overview: SFO has 7+ designated pet relief areas: outdoor pre-security areas at Terminals 1, 2, and 3, plus indoor post-security areas in all four terminals. All are open 24 hours, handicap accessible, and provide water bowls and waste bags.

outdoor.features: Fully fenced, landscaped with trees and shrubs, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls, wheelchair accessible
outdoor.navigation: Follow painted paw-prints on the Arrivals Level terminal curbside walkways

outdoor.features: Fully fenced, landscaped with trees and shrubs, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls, wheelchair accessible
outdoor.navigation: Follow painted paw-prints on the Arrivals Level terminal curbside walkways

outdoor.location: Close to Courtyard 4, Arrivals/Baggage Claim Level
outdoor.features: Fully fenced, landscaped with trees and shrubs, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls, wheelchair accessible
outdoor.navigation: Follow painted paw-prints on the Arrivals Level terminal curbside walkways

indoor.location: B Gates and C Gates (near gate C5)
indoor.features: Artificial turf with built-in drainage, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls
Artificial turf with drainage, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls

indoor.location: F Gates at entrance of F11-F22
indoor.features: Artificial turf with drainage, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls

indoor.location: A Gates and G Gates areas
indoor.features: Artificial turf with built-in drainage, waste bags, trash bins, water bowls

terminal1.name: Alaska Lounge
terminal1.location: Retail street, Level 2, post-security (across from gate B6)

access.status: Alaska MVP Gold 75K and above, oneworld Emerald
access.creditCard: Not directly — requires Alaska status or membership
access.dayPass: Available for purchase (check Alaska Airlines for current pricing)

terminal1.amenities: Hot and cold food options, cocktails, beer, wine, soft drinks, comfortable seating, Wi-Fi, power outlets

terminal1.name: American Airlines Admirals Club
terminal1.location: Adjacent to gate B13, Level 2, post-security

access.status: AA first/business class passengers, oneworld Emerald/Sapphire
access.creditCard: Citi / AAdvantage Executive World Elite Mastercard

terminal1.amenities: Snacks, house drinks, cocktails, wine, beer, shower suites, business center, Wi-Fi, power outlets

terminal1.name: Delta Sky Club
terminal1.location: Adjacent to gate C3, Level 2, post-security
terminal1.hours: Sun-Mon: 4:30 AM-10:30 PM, Tue-Fri: 4:30 AM-12:00 AM, Sat: 4:30 AM-10:30 PM

access.status: Delta Diamond/Platinum/Gold Medallion on same-day Delta flight
access.creditCard: Delta SkyMiles Reserve Amex, Delta SkyMiles Platinum Amex (conditions apply)

terminal1.amenities: Full-service buffet, premium cocktails, wine, beer, complimentary Wi-Fi, digital Wall Street Journal and magazines, power outlets

terminal1.name: The Club SFO

access.creditCard: Priority Pass cards (Chase Sapphire Reserve, Amex Platinum via Priority Pass, Capital One Venture X, etc.)

terminal1.amenities: Fresh seasonal dishes and drinks, spa-style showers ($25 extra), forest-themed children's play area, Wi-Fi, power outlets, comfortable seating

terminal2.name: Air Canada Maple Leaf Lounge
terminal2.hours: 5:30 AM - 7:15 PM daily (hours may vary by day of week)

access.status: Air Canada premium Rouge passengers, business class passengers

terminal2.amenities: Light snacks and beverages, shower facility, newspapers, entertainment, Wi-Fi, power outlets

terminal2.name: American Express Centurion Lounge

access.creditCard: American Express Platinum Card, Centurion Card, Delta SkyMiles Reserve (with restrictions as of 2025)
access.guestPolicy: Platinum cardholders: 2 guests at $50 each or free with $75K+ spend. Centurion cardholders: 2 free guests.

terminal2.amenities: Open kitchen with seasonal menus, full bar with signature cocktails, wine tasting area, family room, high-speed Wi-Fi, business services, phone rooms, premium seating

terminal3.name: United Club (Boarding Area E)

access.status: United premium cabin passengers, Star Alliance Gold

terminal3.amenities: Complimentary food, drinks (including beer, wine, spirits), Wi-Fi, digital magazines, power outlets, TV screens

terminal3.name: United Club (Boarding Area F)

access.status: United premium cabin passengers, Star Alliance Gold

terminal3.amenities: Complimentary food, drinks, Wi-Fi, digital magazines, power outlets

internationalTerminalA.name: Air France-KLM Lounge
internationalTerminalA.location: Adjacent to gate A1, Level 3, post-security
internationalTerminalA.hours: 8:30 AM - 11:30 AM daily (may vary based on flight schedule)

access.status: Air France business/first class, Flying Blue Platinum/Gold

internationalTerminalA.amenities: Self-service meals, beverages, free Wi-Fi, power outlets, USB ports, newspapers, magazines

internationalTerminalA.name: British Airways Lounge
internationalTerminalA.hours: 5:00 AM - 11:30 AM and 1:30 PM - 6:45 PM daily (split hours)

access.status: BA Club World/First passengers, oneworld Sapphire/Emerald

internationalTerminalA.amenities: Relaxed ambiance with cell-free zones, complimentary snacks and beverages, reading materials, Wi-Fi, shower facility

internationalTerminalA.name: Cathay Pacific Lounge
internationalTerminalA.hours: Sun-Fri: 7:25 AM-11:10 AM and 6:55 PM-10:40 PM; Sat: 7:25 AM-11:10 AM and 5:45 PM-10:40 PM

access.status: Cathay Pacific first/business class, oneworld Emerald/Sapphire

internationalTerminalA.amenities: A la carte dining, premium cocktails and beverages, shower suites, massage services, Wi-Fi, comfortable seating

internationalTerminalA.name: China Airlines Lounge
internationalTerminalA.hours: 8:00 PM - 11:00 PM daily (airline passengers); Priority Pass: 8:30 AM - 6:00 PM

access.creditCard: Priority Pass cards (Chase Sapphire Reserve, Capital One Venture X, etc.)
access.dayPass: Via Priority Pass or partner programs

internationalTerminalA.amenities: Premium food, beer, wine, non-smoking environment, Wi-Fi

internationalTerminalA.name: Emirates Lounge
internationalTerminalA.hours: 12:45 PM - 4:45 PM daily (limited hours tied to flight schedule)
internationalTerminalA.amenities: Gourmet cuisine, full-service bars, shower spas, Wi-Fi, premium seating

internationalTerminalA.name: Golden Gate Lounge

access.status: SkyTeam member airline passengers (Air France, Aeromexico, KLM, Qatar Airways, Philippine Airlines, SAS, etc.)
access.dayPass: Via Priority Pass or SkyTeam status

internationalTerminalA.amenities: Disabled access, non-smoking, televisions, newspapers, magazines, beverages, snacks

internationalTerminalA.name: Virgin Atlantic Clubhouse
internationalTerminalA.hours: 9:00 AM - 11:30 PM daily; Priority Pass: 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM and 9:30 PM - 11:30 PM

access.dayPass: Via Priority Pass during designated hours

internationalTerminalA.amenities: Complimentary dining, full bar service, charging facilities, Wi-Fi, premium seating

internationalTerminalG.name: United Polaris Lounge
internationalTerminalG.location: Adjacent to gate G1, Level 2, post-security

access.status: United Polaris business class passengers on international flights, Star Alliance international first/business class
access.creditCard: Not available via credit card alone

internationalTerminalG.amenities: Premium dining with made-to-order meals, signature cocktail bar, shower suites with spa products, individual daybeds, quiet relaxation area, premium Wi-Fi, business services

priorityPassLounges: China Airlines Lounge (International Terminal A — limited hours)
Virgin Atlantic Clubhouse (International Terminal A — limited hours)

wifi.cost: Free — unlimited access with no time limit (re-accept terms every 4 hours)
wifi.coverage: All gate areas, baggage claims, International Terminal Main Hall, and common areas
wifi.speed: Average 173 Mbps download, 100+ Mbps upload — capable of 4K streaming and large file downloads
wifi.premiumOption: No separate premium tier — the free WiFi is already high-speed
wifi.howToConnect: Select '#SFO FREE WIFI' from available networks, open browser, accept terms of service

securityAndScreening.provider: Covenant Aviation Security (private contractor under TSA oversight — SFO does NOT use federal TSA screeners)

tsaPreCheck.benefits: No need to remove shoes, light outerwear, belts; keep laptops and 3-1-1 liquids in carry-on

clear.annualCost: $209/year (discounts for Delta SkyMiles members, United MileagePlus members)
clear.howItWorks: Biometric verification (fingerprint + iris scan) lets you skip the ID check line and go directly to physical screening

globalEntry.notes: Global Entry kiosks in International Arrivals — includes TSA PreCheck benefits

realId.requirement: REAL ID required for domestic air travel as of May 7, 2025
realId.californiaCompliance: Only ~58% of California license/ID holders were REAL ID-compliant as of 2025
realId.identifier: California REAL IDs have a gold bear with white star in the top right corner
realId.alternative: Valid passport, passport card, or military ID also accepted
realId.nonCompliantFee: Starting February 1, 2026: $45 fee for TSA ConfirmID (10-day travel period) if no acceptable ID is presented
realId.howToGet: Apply at California DMV — requires proof of identity (birth certificate/passport), Social Security number, and 2 proofs of California residency

arrivalRecommendation.busyPeriods: 2.5+ hours before departure (especially during 2026 construction/FAA restrictions)

weatherAndDelays.overview: SFO ranks among the top U.S. airports for weather-related delays due to its unique geography. Marine fog from the Pacific regularly reduces visibility, forcing single-runway operations that halve arrival capacity.

2024.delayRate: 29.50% of flights delayed — highest among major U.S. airports in 2024

fogStatistics.mechanism: Pacific Ocean marine layer pushes fog over San Bruno Mountain and across SFO approach paths
fogStatistics.impact: Parallel runway approaches eliminated in fog — capacity drops from ~60 arrivals/hour to ~30 arrivals/hour (50% reduction)
fogStatistics.frequency: Fog or low ceilings affect SFO approaches on roughly 30-40% of summer mornings

worstMonths.summer: June, July, August — fog delays (morning and evening marine layer)
worstMonths.winter: December, January — Pacific storms with rain and reduced visibility
worstMonths.best: September-October (Indian summer) and April-May — fewest weather disruptions

runwayCapacityImpact.clearWeather: ~60 arrivals per hour (side-by-side parallel approaches on 28L/28R)
runwayCapacityImpact.reason: Parallel runways are only 750 feet apart — too close for independent instrument approaches. FAA requires 4,300 ft lateral separation for simultaneous instrument approaches.

faa2026Changes.newRule: FAA permanently banned simultaneous visual parallel approaches at SFO (effective March 2026). Pilots can no longer waive separation requirements in clear weather.
faa2026Changes.capacityReduction: Peak arrival rate cut from ~54 to ~36 flights per hour — a 33% reduction even in clear weather

runway1RightClosure.status: Closed March 30, 2026 for repaving and taxiway improvements
runway1RightClosure.impact: All operations concentrated on 28L/28R during closure

faa2026Changes.combinedDelayProjection: ~25% of arriving flights expected to face delays of 30+ minutes during overlap period (March-October 2026), up from ~15% before the FAA rule change
faa2026Changes.permanentImpact: The FAA parallel approach restriction is permanent — ongoing capacity constraint even after runway 1R reopens in October 2026

construction2026.status: Major construction and FAA restrictions creating significant 2026 disruptions

projects.name: Terminal 3 West Modernization
projects.architects: Gensler and TEF Design (both San Francisco-based)
projects.scope: 200,000 sq ft expansion + 650,000 sq ft renovation
features: New six-story building for airline lounges and operational offices
Redesigned check-in area with improved baggage handling
International arrivals capability for up to four gates
projects.workforce: 500-600 workers at peak construction; $173M+ in contracts targeted for Local Business Enterprises
projects.temporaryInfrastructure: Prefabricated passenger corridors (1,000-foot West Corridor and 400-foot East Corridor) fabricated into 11 modules, transported across airfield over 11 consecutive nights to minimize disruption
projects.travelerImpact: Gate changes and detours for United Airlines domestic flights in Terminal 3. Check airline for specific gate assignments.

projects.name: Runway 1R/19L Repaving
projects.impact: All operations on 28L/28R during closure. Combined with FAA parallel approach ban, delays projected at 25% of flights experiencing 30+ minute delays.

completedProjects: Harvey Milk Terminal 1 ($2.4B) — COMPLETE 2024. Full modernization with new Boarding Areas B and C.
Terminal 2 ($383M renovation) — COMPLETE 2011. First LEED Gold airport terminal in U.S.
International Terminal ($2.4B) — COMPLETE December 2000. Largest international terminal in North America when built.
New Control Tower ($102M) — COMPLETE October 2016. Earthquake-resistant, located between Terminals 1 and 2.
construction2026.travelAdvice2026: March-October 2026 is the peak disruption period due to simultaneous runway closure and FAA capacity restriction. Book morning flights when possible, build extra buffer time, check flight status frequently, and consider alternative airports (OAK, SJC) for time-sensitive travel.

parkingLots.name: DoubleTree SFO North Bayfront
parkingLots.notes: Budget option. Hotel parking lot available to non-guests.

parkingLots.name: Travelodge SFO Airport Parking
parkingLots.address: 326 S Airport Blvd, South San Francisco, CA 94080
parkingLots.notes: Budget-friendly option with strong reviews. Hotel parking lot.

parkingLots.name: Aloft Westin SFO Airport Parking
parkingLots.notes: Closest off-site lot at 0.8 mi. Best seller on multiple platforms. Minimum 2-day booking.

parkingLots.name: Hyatt Regency SFO Parking
parkingLots.notes: Hotel parking with upscale amenities. Park-sleep-fly packages available (5 days self-parking included with overnight stay).

parkingLots.name: The Westin SFO Parking
parkingLots.notes: Close proximity (0.9 mi). Frequently sells out — book in advance. Minimum 2-day booking.

parkingLots.name: Park N Fly SFO
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle to SFO terminals, runs frequently
parkingLots.address: 160 Produce Ave, South San Francisco, CA 94080
parkingLots.notes: Highest-rated SFO lot (5.0/5 with 971 reviews). Premium pricing but excellent service. Established airport parking operator with locations nationwide.

parkingLots.name: Crowne Plaza SFO Parking
parkingLots.notes: Premium hotel parking. Park-sleep-fly packages available (7 days complimentary parking with overnight stay). Highest off-site rate — only worth it with hotel package.

shuttleInfo: All off-site lots include free shuttle service to SFO terminals. On-airport Long-Term Garage connects via free AirTrain Blue Line (24/7 service, every 4 minutes). Rental Car Center also connected via AirTrain Blue Line.
distanceFromCity: 12 miles south of downtown San Francisco, roughly 25-45 min by car depending on US-101 traffic. BART takes ~30 minutes.

keyDistances.toDowntownSF: ~12 miles, 25-45 min by car, ~30 min by BART
keyDistances.toOakland: ~30 miles, 40-60 min by car, ~50 min by BART
keyDistances.toSanJose: ~32 miles, 35-50 min by car, ~60 min by Caltrain (via Millbrae)
keyDistances.competitiveAdvantage: SFO is the only Bay Area airport with direct BART rail connection. No parking shuttle or car needed — take AirTrain to BART to downtown SF in 30 min for $8.95.

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near SFO — stay the night before, park free for your trip, shuttle to terminal.

bartRoundTrip.note: BART from downtown SF: $8.95 each way. Free AirTrain connection at SFO.

savingsComparison.keyDifference: SFO has a major advantage: direct BART rail connection ($8.95 from downtown SF, 30 min). Many Bay Area travelers skip parking entirely and take BART. For those who drive, off-site lots start at $8/day vs $27-39/day on-airport.

liveSources._description: Authoritative URLs to scrape for fresh data when generating articles. Grouped by topic. All verified 2026-04-03.
